tasks.title: Update --class flag example from lang-go to coding/go
tasks.description: Update --class flag example from lang-go to coding/go
tasks.body: In cmd/task/add.go line 181, the --class flag help text reads 'Task class override (e.g., lang-go)'. The actual class keys in production config use the 'coding/go' format (see internal/config/config.go:133 and cmd/task/task_test.go). Change the example to 'e.g., coding/go'. Run 'go build ./...' to confirm compilation.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in update-help-text is complete and correct

breadcrumbs.text: Updated --class flag help text example from lang-go to coding/go in cmd/task/add.go

Audit reviewed 1 file (cmd/task/add.go, line 181). PASS. The change is a single-line fix updating the --class flag help text example from 'lang-go' to 'coding/go', matching the hierarchical class key format used in production config. Build passes, all 20 test packages green. No findings introduced by this node. Remaining 'lang-go' references in test fixtures and code comments are pre-existing test data for the flat-key format, which the system still supports; not stale help text.

audit.result_summary: Fixed stale --class flag help text in cmd/task/add.go: updated the example value from 'lang-go' (flat format) to 'coding/go' (hierarchical format) so the CLI help reflects the actual class key convention used in production configuration. Single-line change, no regressions, all tests pass.

task-0001.objective: Fix stale example in --class flag help text
task-0001.what_happened: Changed the example value in cmd/task/add.go line 181 from 'lang-go' to 'coding/go' to match the actual class key format used in production config.
